summary:
Sealants are most effective in arresting noncavitated carious lesions, which prevents the development of recurrent caries by intervening at an early stage. They do not treat established cavity formations or restore margins where secondary caries typically occur, making their primary benefit aligned with preemptive control of incipient decay.  

(End summary)

(End summary). Answer choice: A
